messages.content: does it by defualt install it in my user directory, or is it just becuase it already exists there? can we remove it there and test again?

content.text:
It always installs to the user directory — `getClaudeSettingsPath()` hardcodes `~/.claude/settings.json` at `claude-settings.ts:26`. There's no project-level option.

Let me remove the hook and test.

Run rudel enable after removing hook

content.content:
$ bun run src/bin/cli.ts enable
Auto-upload hook enabled in /Users/marc/.claude/settings.json

content.text: It works — the hook was added back to `~/.claude/settings.json`. So yes, it always targets the user-level settings file by default. The reason it "didn't do anything" before was simply because the hook was already present.

messages.content: oh that is bad, we should ALWAYS try to target the root of the current repository? or going up from the current directory until there is a `.claude` directory. and if there is none, the root of the current repository.

content.thinking:
The user wants to change the behavior so that `rudel enable` installs the hook in the project-level `.claude/settings.json` instead of the user-level one. The logic should be:

1. Walk up from the current directory until finding a `.claude` directory
2. If none found, use the root of the current git repository
3. Write to `.claude/settings.json` in that location

Let me update `claude-settings.ts` to implement this.

content.text: Makes sense — project-level hooks are better than polluting the user's global settings. Let me update `claude-settings.ts`.

content.text: It now targets the project-level settings. Let me verify it was written correctly:

Working correctly — the rudel hook was appended to the existing `SessionEnd` array in the project-level `.claude/settings.json` at `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/.claude/settings.json`, preserving all existing hooks.

Let me also verify `disable` works and clean up the test entry:

input.description: Run rudel disable to test removal
Run rudel disable to test removal

content.content:
$ bun run src/bin/cli.ts disable
Auto-upload hook removed from /Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/.claude/settings.json

content.text:
Both `enable` and `disable` work correctly with project-level settings. The rudel hook was cleanly removed while preserving all other hooks.

Summary of the change: `getClaudeSettingsPath()` now walks up from `cwd` looking for an existing `.claude/` directory, and falls back to the git repo root if none is found — instead of always targeting `~/.claude/settings.json`.
